title: pelargonium rubidum
intro: Pelargonium rubidum, also known as the Red-leaved Geranium, is a species of flowering plant that belongs to the Geraniaceae family. Traditionally, the leaves and flowers of Pelargonium rubidum have been used in herbal medicine, particularly in South Africa, where it is native, due to its medicinal properties, including anti-inflammatory and antimicrobial properties. The health benefits of Pelargonium rubidum include its potential to alleviate symptoms of colds, flu, and sore throats, as well as its ability to soothe digestive issues and reduce stress. Pelargonium rubidum can be prepared as a herbal tea, infusion, or tincture, and is often combined with other herbs to enhance its medicinal properties.
